**Mjøndalen IF Fotball** – commonly known as **Mjøndalen IF**, and colloquially as **Mjøndar'n** or **MIF** – is the [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") department of [Mjøndalen IF](/wiki/Mj%C3%B8ndalen_IF "Mjøndalen IF"). Founded in 1910, the club is located in [Mjøndalen](/wiki/Mj%C3%B8ndalen "Mjøndalen"), [Buskerud](/wiki/Buskerud "Buskerud"), Norway. The team plays its home matches in the 4,350-capacity [Consto Arena](/wiki/Consto_Arena "Consto Arena"). Mjøndalen competes in [2. divisjon](/wiki/Norwegian_Second_Division "Norwegian Second Division"), the third tier in the [Norwegian football league system](/wiki/Norwegian_football_league_system "Norwegian football league system"), having been relegated from the [Norwegian First Division](/wiki/Norwegian_First_Division "Norwegian First Division") at the end of the 2025 season.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mj%C3%B8ndalen_IF_Fotball&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### First era

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mj%C3%B8ndalen_IF_Fotball&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: First era")]

The club was founded in 1910. Mjøndalen reached the final of the [1924 Norwegian Cup](/wiki/1924_Norwegian_Football_Cup "1924 Norwegian Football Cup") where they lost 3–0 against [Odd](/wiki/Odds_BK "Odds BK"). In the 1930s, Mjøndalen won the [Norwegian Cup](/wiki/Norwegian_Football_Cup "Norwegian Football Cup") three times; [1933](/wiki/1933_Norwegian_Football_Cup "1933 Norwegian Football Cup"), [1934](/wiki/1934_Norwegian_Football_Cup "1934 Norwegian Football Cup") and [1937](/wiki/1937_Norwegian_Football_Cup "1937 Norwegian Football Cup"). In the same decade, they also lost in the finals thrice; in [1931](/wiki/1931_Norwegian_Football_Cup "1931 Norwegian Football Cup"), [1936](/wiki/1936_Norwegian_Football_Cup "1936 Norwegian Football Cup") and [1938](/wiki/1938_Norwegian_Football_Cup "1938 Norwegian Football Cup").

### Second era and European debut

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mj%C3%B8ndalen_IF_Fotball&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Second era and European debut")]

As runners-up in the [1968 Norwegian Cup](/wiki/1968_Norwegian_Football_Cup "1968 Norwegian Football Cup"), Mjøndalen men's football team represented Norway in the [European Cup Winners' Cup](/wiki/European_Cup_Winners%27_Cup "European Cup Winners' Cup") in the following [1969–70](/wiki/1969%E2%80%9370_European_Cup_Winners%27_Cup "1969–70 European Cup Winners' Cup") season. They were eliminated in the first round by [Cardiff City](/wiki/Cardiff_City_F.C. "Cardiff City F.C.") who defeated Mjøndalen 12–2 on aggregate. After being promoted to the [top division](/wiki/Eliteserien "Eliteserien") in 1971, Mjøndalen regularly played on the top-tier of Norwegian football in the 1970s and 1980s, and finished second in 1976 and 1986. Finishing runners-up in the league qualified them to represent Norway in the [UEFA Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Cup "UEFA Cup") the following season.

### Middle years

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mj%C3%B8ndalen_IF_Fotball&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Middle years")]

The last round of the 1976 season saw Mjøndalen beat [SK Brann](/wiki/SK_Brann "SK Brann"), a result which would have awarded them the league title if [Lillestrøm SK](/wiki/Lillestr%C3%B8m_SK "Lillestrøm SK") had lost their game — which they did not. However, Mjøndalen's silver medals saw the team qualify for the [1977–78 UEFA Cup](/wiki/1977%E2%80%9378_UEFA_Cup "1977–78 UEFA Cup"), where they lost the first round against [Bayern Munich](/wiki/FC_Bayern_Munich "FC Bayern Munich") 0–12 on aggregate. After Mjøndalen's second silver medals in the 1986 league season, they were relegated the following season and were not promoted back to the top level until [1992](/wiki/1992_Tippeligaen "1992 Tippeligaen"). A highlight from the years in lower divisions was their meeting with German side [Werder Bremen](/wiki/SV_Werder_Bremen "SV Werder Bremen") in the first round of the [1987–88 UEFA Cup](/wiki/1987%E2%80%9388_UEFA_Cup "1987–88 UEFA Cup"). Mjøndalen lost 1–5 on aggregate, but their 1–0 win away at Bremen, remain their only victory in [UEFA competitions](/wiki/UEFA_competitions "UEFA competitions"). After their 1992 top-flight comeback season, Mjøndalen was relegated, had financial challenges, and participated in the second, third and fourth tier of Norwegian football for a number of seasons.

### Recent years

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mj%C3%B8ndalen_IF_Fotball&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Recent years")]

Mjøndalen had a successful [2008](/wiki/2008_2._divisjon "2008 2. divisjon") in [2. divisjon](/wiki/2._divisjon "2. divisjon"), and returned to [1. divisjon](/wiki/1._divisjon "1. divisjon") for the 2009 season. The club qualified to compete in the promotion knock out stage of 1. divisjon in both [2012](/wiki/2012_1._divisjon "2012 1. divisjon") and [2013](/wiki/2013_1._divisjon "2013 1. divisjon"), and finally earned promotion to the [Tippeligaen](/wiki/Eliteserien "Eliteserien") with a successful [2014](/wiki/2014_1._divisjon "2014 1. divisjon") play-off result against Brann.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Mjøndalen's return to [Tippeligaen for 2015](/wiki/2015_Tippeligaen "2015 Tippeligaen") marked their first appearance in the top tier in 22 years. The team finished in 15th place — second from bottom — and was relegated to the [2016 1. divisjon](/wiki/2016_1._divisjon "2016 1. divisjon"). In 2018, Mjøndalen finished in second place in the [2018 1. divisjon](/wiki/2018_1._divisjon "2018 1. divisjon") and was promoted to [Eliteserien](/wiki/Eliteserien "Eliteserien"). In the following season, Mjøndalen succeeded in retaining their spot in the top flight through winning on the last day of the season.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mj%C3%B8ndalen_IF_Fotball&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-league_naming_3-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-league_naming_3-1) [***c***](#cite_ref-league_naming_3-2) 1. divisjon was the name of the top tier of Norwegian league football from 1963 until 1990. The top tier changed its name to the sponsor-affiliated name *Tippeligaen* ahead of the 1990 season. The second tier formerly known as 2. divisjon inherited the name [1. divisjon](/wiki/1._divisjon "1. divisjon") ahead of the 1991 season. The third tier, formerly known as 3. divisjon was named [2. divisjon](/wiki/2._divisjon "2. divisjon") ahead of the 1991 season and the names of all the lower divisions were adjusted accordingly. Effecting 2017, the top tier is named Eliteserien.
